Dear Mark,

Thank you for telling me that my post on my personal social media has been concerned by a certain group of people. By writing this letter, I would like to clarify the message I posted as I believe it is an misunderstanding to some extent.

I have recently uploaded an instagram story on my personal account of my own cat, and writing "求所有中國人原地自爆 滾回你們的中國 唔好再輸出病毒". Personally, I believe that the United Kingdom is a free country with freedom of speech which protects everyone in this country have their right to express their political views.
The English meaning of my instagram story, was stating that all people who carries the coronavirus from China should stay in China. For those who has already gone to foreign countries, They should head back to where they were instead of exporting the virus causing another outbreak in other countries. The post was only addressing the people who were in the high risk area, and especially addressing those who took Paracetomol before the border checks when they entered to foreign countries, according to the news. I regret that my post has been misunderstood by other Chinese people as they do not speak Cantonese.
In addition, I would like to express my concern to some nasty messages I have received after I posted the story. Those message including some of the terribly rude words such as: "傻逼香港人(silly cu*t Hongkongers" "英國的狗(British dog, as Hong Kong was a British colony)" "Did SARS come out from your mother's cu*t? (Hong Kong had a serious outbreak of SARS in 2003 imported from China)" "Your mother will die by car accident tomorrow" "your whole family is getting pneumonia tonight" "your whole family is having dinner in the hell for the CNY".
In my opinion, those cursing messages that I received are unacceptable to anybody no matter what political orientation one is for. As a university of a democratic society, I hope that the school would protect my freedom of speech from being verbally abused, instead of commanding me to delete my opinions. It would be appreciated if our school could educate the people the definition and the importance of freedom of speech for those who have no experience of it in their home countries.

Best regards

I have recently uploaded an instagram story on my personal account of my own cat, and writing "求所有中國人原地自爆 滾回你們的中國 唔好再輸出病毒". Personally, I believe that the United Kingdom is a free country with freedom of speech which protects everyone in this country have their right to express their political views.
The English meaning of my instagram story, was stating that all people who carries the coronavirus from China should stay in China. For those who has already gone to foreign countries, They should head back to where they were instead of exporting the virus causing another outbreak in other countries. The post was only addressing the people who were in the high risk area, and especially addressing those who took Paracetomol before the border checks when they entered to foreign countries, according to the news. I regret that my post has been misunderstood by other Chinese people as they do not speak Cantonese.
In addition, I would like to express my concern to some nasty messages I have received after I posted the story. Those message including some of the terribly rude words such as: "傻逼香港人(silly cu*t Hongkongers" "英國的狗(British dog, as Hong Kong was a British colony)" "Did SARS come out from your mother's cu*t? (Hong Kong had a serious outbreak of SARS in 2003 imported from China)" "Your mother will die by car accident tomorrow" "your whole family is getting pneumonia tonight" "your whole family is having dinner in the hell for the CNY".
In my opinion, those cursing messages that I received are unacceptable to anybody no matter what political orientation one is for. As a university of a democratic society, I hope that the school would protect my freedom of speech from being verbally abused, instead of commanding me to delete my opinions. It would be appreciated if our school could educate the people the definition and the importance of freedom of speech for those who have no experience of it in their home countries.

Best regards

Dear Mark,

Thank you for telling me that my post on my personal social media has been concerned by a certain group of people. By writing this letter, I would like to clarify the message I posted as I believe it is an misunderstanding to some extent.

I have recently uploaded an instagram story on my personal account of my own cat, and writing "求所有中國人原地自爆 滾回你們的中國 唔好再輸出病毒". Personally, I believe that the United Kingdom is a free country with freedom of speech which protects everyone in this country have their right to express their political views.
The English meaning of my instagram story, was stating that all people who carries the coronavirus from China should stay in China. For those who has already gone to foreign countries, They should head back to where they were instead of exporting the virus causing another outbreak in other countries. The post was only addressing the people who were in the high risk area, and especially addressing those who took Paracetomol before the border checks when they entered to foreign countries, according to the news. I regret that my post has been misunderstood by other Chinese people as they do not speak Cantonese.
In addition, I would like to express my concern to some nasty messages I have received after I posted the story. Those message including some of the terribly rude words such as: "傻逼香港人(silly cu*t Hongkongers" "英國的狗(British dog, as Hong Kong was a British colony)" "Did SARS come out from your mother's cu*t? (Hong Kong had a serious outbreak of SARS in 2003 imported from China)" "Your mother will die by car accident tomorrow" "your whole family is getting pneumonia tonight" "your whole family is having dinner in the hell for the CNY".
In my opinion, those cursing messages that I received are unacceptable to anybody no matter what political orientation one is for. As a university of a democratic society, I hope that the school would protect my freedom of speech from being verbally abused, instead of commanding me to delete my opinions. It would be appreciated if our school could educate the people the definition and the importance of freedom of speech for those who have no experience of it in their home countries.

Best regards

Dear Mark,

Thank you for telling me that my post on my personal social media has been concerned by a certain group of people. By writing this letter, I would like to clarify the message I posted as I believe it is an misunderstanding to some extent.

I have recently uploaded an instagram story on my personal account of my own cat, and writing "求所有中國人原地自爆 滾回你們的中國 唔好再輸出病毒". Personally, I believe that the United Kingdom is a free country with freedom of speech which protects everyone in this country have their right to express their political views.
The English meaning of my instagram story, was stating that all people who carries the coronavirus from China should stay in China. For those who has already gone to foreign countries, They should head back to where they were instead of exporting the virus causing another outbreak in other countries. The post was only addressing the people who were in the high risk area, and especially addressing those who took Paracetomol before the border checks when they entered to foreign countries, according to the news. I regret that my post has been misunderstood by other Chinese people as they do not speak Cantonese.
In addition, I would like to express my concern to some nasty messages I have received after I posted the story. Those message including some of the terribly rude words such as: "傻逼香港人(silly cu*t Hongkongers" "英國的狗(British dog, as Hong Kong was a British colony)" "Did SARS come out from your mother's cu*t? (Hong Kong had a serious outbreak of SARS in 2003 imported from China)" "Your mother will die by car accident tomorrow" "your whole family is getting pneumonia tonight" "your whole family is having dinner in the hell for the CNY".
In my opinion, those cursing messages that I received are unacceptable to anybody no matter what political orientation one is for. As a university of a democratic society, I hope that the school would protect my freedom of speech from being verbally abused, instead of commanding me to delete my opinions. It would be appreciated if our school could educate the people the definition and the importance of freedom of speech for those who have no experience of it in their home countries.

Best regards
